title: lathyrus pannonicus subsp. velebiticus
intro: Lathyrus pannonicus subsp. velebiticus is a subspecies of the perennial legume Lathyrus pannonicus, commonly known as the Balkan peavine, a plant native to the mountainous regions of the Balkan Peninsula. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and supporting digestive health. Lathyrus pannonicus subsp. velebiticus can be prepared in various herbal forms, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be consumed as a tea or added to other herbal remedies.
